name: Progyny, Inc.
business: Progyny is a global leader in women's health and family building solutions. It launched its fertility benefits solution in 2016 and now serves over 590 employers with at least 1,000 covered lives. The company contracts with employers to provide women's health and family building benefits to their employees and covered dependents.
sellsTo: Labor populations under the Taft-Hartley Act

exposures.sentence: Our solutions empower members with concierge support, coaching, education, and digital tools; provide access to a premier network of fertility and women’s health specialists who use the latest science and technologies; drive optimal clinical outcomes; and reduce healthcare costs.

If our information technology systems, or those of third parties with whom we do business, including our provider clinics, specialty pharmacies or other vendors, lag, fail or suffer cybersecurity breaches, we may experience a material disruption of our services or suffer a loss or inappropriate disclosure of confidential information, which could materially impact our business and results of operations.
Our use of artificial intelligence may subject us to new or heightened legal, regulatory, ethical, operational or other challenges.

catalysts.event: Entered into a $200 million revolving credit facility
catalysts.sentence: On July 1, 2025, we entered into a revolving credit facility (the Facility) pursuant to a Credit Agreement (the Credit Agreement) with the lenders and issuing banks, party thereto and JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A., as administrative agent, collateral agent, and swing line lender.

catalysts.event: Authorized a $200 million share repurchase program
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, our Board of Directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $200 million in shares of common stock (the November 2025 share repurchase program).

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 106 million potential covered lives in total in the addressable market.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 9,000 employers in the United States who have a minimum of 1,000 employees, who together with the Taft-Hartley populations and federal government populations, represent approximately 106 million potential covered lives in total.
